opp.name: Charging_SRPM_June '26
opp.next_step: 6/30/26 : Sharing Zycus overview material and reconnect with Eric to define the initial greenfield scope for Charging NC around SRPM, Intake, CLM, and Spend Analysis. Keeping prospect nurtured by inviting to Horizon. To Follow up in November.
opp.ais_why:
• Charging NC is at Qualified on an SRPM motion with only Merlin scoped and no discovery description recorded.
• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and there are no pain points or AI sentiment captured.
• Product scope suggests openness to Zycus AI-enabled SRPM, but evidence is too thin for more than exploratory appetite.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case has been documented yet. This is a brand-new greenfield opportunity (created 2026-06-30) and discovery has not yet occurred. The Next Step indicates the rep is scoping initial product areas (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis) but no buyer pain, cost impact, cycle time, or savings target has been articulated. The immediate move is to run a discovery call with Eric Rabuse to uncover the business problem driving the evaluation and quantify the financial or operational impact (cost savings, FTE reduction, cycle time improvement, compliance risk). Without a quantified value case, progression beyond Qualified will stall.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Eric Rabuse is the only named contact (SF task 2026-06-30), but his role, seniority, access to decision-makers, and willingness to advocate internally are unknown. He has not yet responded to the 2026-06-30 outreach. A champion in an enterprise deal must have credibility with the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker, understand the business case, and actively drive internal momentum (e.g., scheduling next steps, sharing internal docs, defending Zycus against competitors). The immediate move is to assess Eric's role and influence during the discovery call, and if he is mid-level or passive, identify and cultivate a senior business or procurement leader who can sponsor the evaluation and provide internal navigation.

sources: No Next Step or Task mentions of competing vendors
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. No Salesforce competitor fields are populated, no buyer calls have occurred, and the Next Step does not reference other vendors. For a SRPM/CLM/Spend Analysis scope, likely competitors include SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, and Jaggaer. The discovery call must surface: Is Charging NC evaluating other vendors? Are they running a formal RFP with multiple shortlisted vendors? Are they currently using an incumbent solution (and if so, why are they looking to replace it)? Is this a greenfield build or a replacement? If competitors are present, the wedge is Merlin's AI-driven negotiation intelligence and supplier onboarding automation, positioned as the differentiator in Phase 2 discussions. If sole-sourced, confirm it explicitly to de-risk competitive displacement later.

sources: SF Next Step 2026-06-30: scoping SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis
identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain driving this evaluation has not been articulated. The product scope (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis) implies process inefficiencies in supplier management, contract handling, or spend visibility, but no buyer has stated the pain in their own words. Is Charging NC managing suppliers in spreadsheets? Are contracts stuck in email chains? Is spend data siloed across systems? Are compliance risks or audit failures driving urgency? The discovery call with Eric must uncover and document the pain in the buyer's language, quantify it (e.g., hours wasted, dollars at risk, number of suppliers), and identify the pain owner (the person who feels the problem most acutely and will champion a solution).

sources: No SF fields populated for legal/contracting
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are completely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or SI/partner involvement has occurred. This is appropriate for a 2-day-old Qualified deal. As the deal progresses toward Shortlisted and Vendor Selected, the discovery and demo phases should surface: Does Charging NC require a new MSA or do they have an existing framework? Is InfoSec review required (especially for Merlin AI integration)? Will a systems integrator or partner be involved in implementation? What is the procurement approval flow (e.g., does purchasing require competitive bids)? Clarifying these in the Formal Evaluation stage will prevent late-cycle bottlenecks.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is not yet identified. Eric Rabuse is the only named contact (from a Salesforce task 2026-06-30) but his title, role, and budget authority are unknown. No contact roles have been established, and no buyer calls have occurred to surface who controls commercial decisions and pricing approval. For a $100K deal, the EB is likely a VP or Director-level procurement, finance, or operations leader. The next move is to ask Eric directly during the discovery call who will own the business case and approve the budget, and to request an introduction to that person early in the cycle.

sources: SF Next Step 2026-06-30: November follow-up planned
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. No buyer calls, no documented approval chain, and no OpportunityContactRole records exist yet. The Next Step references a November follow-up, implying a long-cycle evaluation, but the actual process (discovery → demo → RFI/RFP → shortlist → commercials → EB sign-off) has not been mapped. The discovery call must clarify: Are they running a formal RFP? Is this sole-sourced? Who participates in evaluation (procurement, IT, business users)? What is the approval chain (single sign-off or multi-tier)? When do they need to make a decision to meet their 2027-07-31 target? Without this, the 394-day runway could compress unexpectedly if the buyer's internal timeline is shorter.

sources: SF Next Step 2026-06-30: scoping SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis
No Avoma calls or RFP/RFI documentation
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria have not been documented. The product scope (SRPM, Intake, CLM, Spend Analysis) suggests functional requirements around supplier management, contract lifecycle, and spend visibility, but no structured evaluation criteria, RFP scorecard, or must-have capabilities have been shared. This is expected for a 2-day-old Qualified deal. The discovery call should surface what Charging NC will measure vendors against (e.g., ease of integration with their ERP, supplier onboarding speed, contract redlining automation, AI/Merlin capabilities for negotiation intelligence, reporting depth) and whether they are running a formal RFP or RFI process.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AI fit signal not assessed. No AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, or AIS_Why__c populated. Merlin is in product scope, but buyer's AI appetite is unknown. Discovery call should explore: Is Charging NC investing in AI/automation? Do they have AI initiatives in procurement or sourcing? Are negotiation intelligence or supplier risk prediction compelling? This will determine whether Merlin is a differentiator or a nice-to-have.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 4 is carried by momentum 50 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 50 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 44 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 4 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (4), nudged by commitment 44 and momentum 50, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

items.action: Secure discovery call with Eric Rabuse within 14 days to uncover business problem, quantify pain (cost, FTE, cycle time), map stakeholders (Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, org chart), assess AI appetite, and surface decision process and competition. This is the activation for all MEDDPICC elements.
items.trigger: Rep-initiated outreach 2026-06-30; Eric has not yet responded
items.expected_effect: Validates buyer interest, begins qualification, establishes next milestone (demo or RFI), and de-risks single-threading by surfacing additional stakeholders. Without this call, the deal cannot progress beyond Qualified.

items.action: Multi-thread by requesting introductions to senior procurement, finance, or operations leaders during the discovery call. Identify and engage the Economic Buyer and a potential Champion early in the cycle.
items.trigger: Single-threaded: only Eric Rabuse named in SF task 2026-06-30
items.expected_effect: De-risks single-threading and builds access to budget authority and internal advocacy. Without senior engagement, the deal stalls at mid-level and cannot progress to commercials or EB sign-off.

items.action: Assess AI appetite and Merlin fit during discovery call. If Charging NC has AI initiatives or interest in negotiation intelligence and supplier automation, position Merlin as the Phase 2 differentiator and request AIS scoring from product/sales leadership.
items.trigger: Merlin in product scope (SF Products__c) but no AIS signal or buyer AI interest documented
items.expected_effect: If AI appetite is strong, Merlin becomes the wedge in competitive situations and accelerates decision criteria alignment. If weak, de-emphasize Merlin and focus on core SRPM/CLM/Spend Analysis value.

items.action: Confirm Horizon event attendance with Eric or a senior Charging NC leader. Use Horizon as a soft nurture touchpoint and an opportunity to introduce executive sponsors and deepen champion relationships.
items.trigger: Horizon invite extended per SF Next Step 2026-06-30
items.expected_effect: Keeps Charging NC engaged during long-cycle evaluation and provides executive access outside of formal deal milestones. Horizon attendance signals buyer commitment and interest.

items.action: If Eric does not respond by mid-July, escalate outreach to the deal owner's manager for executive connect. Request manager to reach out to a senior Charging NC leader (VP Procurement, CFO, or COO) to validate interest and restart engagement.
items.trigger: Rep-initiated outreach 2026-06-30; if no buyer response within 14-21 days, engagement risk increases
items.expected_effect: Prevents deal from going dark and validates whether Charging NC is genuinely interested or if the opportunity should be deprioritized. Executive-level outreach can restart stalled early-stage deals by reaching above the initial contact.

Pace required: discovery depth, demo, and RFI positioning over next 60-90 days to progress toward Formal Evaluation. Rep-initiated outreach 2026-06-30; awaiting buyer engagement.
